# CV

# [Madeline Cunningham]{.underline}

*maddiecunningham\@live.com*

## Education

-   Colorado State University

Water Resource Data, PSM M.S.

In Progress

-   University of California, Santa Barbara

Aquatic Biology B.S., Distinction in Major\
GPA: 3.57 \| Dean’s List

-   San Diego Community College\
    Biology A.A.\
    GPA: 3.52 \| Honors List\

    \
    Experience\
    \
    San Diego Zoo Wildlife Alliance — Research Assistant\
    March 2024 – Present\
    \

    -   Responsible for daily husbandry of 250+ endangered frogs across life stages\
    -   Led \~200 field surveys supporting habitat assessments, health, density, and release efforts\
    -   Contributed to development of reproductive health research methods\
    -   Developed project comparing field vs laboratory water quality analysis\
    -   Collected biological data through expert handling and species care\
        \
        Moorea Coral Reef LTER — Lab Assistant II\
        November 2023 – November 2024\
        \
    -   Build orthomosaic reef models using Metashape, TagLab, Excel, Photoshop\
    -   Identify 100+ marine species including algae, coral, and fauna\
    -   Classify benthic ecology datasets and manage large ecological datasets\
        \
        Moorea Coral Reef LTER — REU Intern\
        June 2023 – August 2023\
        \
    -   Conducted coral reef resilience research (phase shifts, disease, nutrients)\
    -   Completed 100+ AAUS scientific dives and 25 photogrammetry dives\
    -   Processed 300+ hours of video data and managed 200+ dataset entries\
    -   Selected as 1 of 3 interns\
        \
        National Park Service — Biological Field Technician\
        June 2022 – September 2022\
        \
    -   Removed invasive freshwater fish in Kings Canyon National Park\
    -   Surveyed endangered frog populations in remote backcountry conditions\
    -   Managed field equipment and gear maintenance\
    -   Awarded STAR Award for field performance and leadership\
        \
        UCSB Burkepile Lab — Research Intern\
        April 2022 – June 2023\
        \
    -   Studied coral reef community ecology and algal phase shifts\
    -   Built orthomosaics using Metashape and TagLab\
    -   Processed \~150 algal samples and supported senior thesis research\
    -   Wrote thesis: "Nutrient Gradient Impacts on Coral Reefs in French Polynesia"\
        \
        Channel Islands Marine & Wildlife Institute — Volunteer\
        March 2022 – June 2022\
        \
    -   Rehabilitated pinnipeds (up to 8 at a time)\
    -   Assisted in rescue, care, feeding, and medication administration\
    -   Contributed to successful release of 10 rehabilitated animals\
        \
        Reef Check California — Intern\
        September 2021 – May 2022\
        \
    -   Collected and cleaned kelp forest ecological data\
    -   Entered multi-year datasets into Excel and Google Drive\
    -   Observed marine ecological interactions in California kelp systems\
        \
        Outreach & Leadership\
        \
        Letters to a Pre-Scientist — Mentor\
        2023–2024\
    -   Mentored middle school student in STEM career exploration\

    UCSB Scuba Club — Vice President\
    2022–2023\

    -   Led 400+ members in diving trips and training\
    -   Co-created scholarship for PADI + AAUS certification\
        \
        Private Tutor\
        2021–2022\
    -   Supported student with Down Syndrome in coursework and study skills\
        \
        Everyday California — Tour Guide\
        2018–2021\
    -   Led kayak/snorkel tours in La Jolla Underwater Canyon\
        \
        Publications & Presentations\
        \
    -   Kopecky et al. (2024). \*Global Change Biology\*, 30(9): e17504\
    -   UCSB Undergraduate Research Symposium (2023):\
        \*Examining the Effects of Anthropogenic Nutrient Gradients on Coral Reefs in French Polynesia\*\
        \
        Awards\
        \
    -   REU Recipient (2022)\
    -   STAR Award – National Park Service (2022)\
    -   Bahamian Conservation Award (2019)\
    -   Dean’s Honors (multiple quarters)\
    -   Distinction in Major – UCSB (2023)\
        \
        Certifications\
        \
    -   CPR, First Aid, O2 (exp. 2025)\
    -   Wilderness First Aid (exp. 2026)\
    -   AAUS Scientific Diver\
    -   PADI Rescue Diver\
    -   Yoga Alliance 200hr\
        \
        Skills\
        \
    -   R programming, Excel, Google Drive, Microsoft Office\
    -   Agisoft Metashape, TagLab, Photoshop, Access\
    -   Data management & ecological analysis\
    -   Field diving (AAUS), SCUBA, sampling techniques\
    -   Leadership, communication, scientific writing
